claims.claim: Public – all visitors can view the page, including those who are not signed in. This is the default for new pages.
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.Public() => new(false) means no auth required. CreateWebPageTests.cs line 212 confirms parameters.AccessSettings.IsSecured == false for new page creation.
claims.docFact: AREA-002: ContentAccessSettings supports three tiers: Public (no auth), Secured (any authenticated), SecuredWithRoles (specific roles)

claims.claim: Signed-in members only – only visitors who are signed in to the website (i.e., registered members) can view the page.
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.Secured() => new(true) — requires authentication, no specific roles. ContentAccessSettings.cs line 39.
claims.docFact: AREA-002: Three-tier content access model: Secured (any authenticated user)

claims.claim: Specific member roles – only signed-in members who belong to one or more selected member roles can view the page. Members who are signed in but do not have the required role cannot access the content.
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.SecuredWithRoles(IEnumerable<int> requiredMemberRoleIDs) at line 80 of ContentAccessSettings.cs. Requires at least one role ID (throws ArgumentException if empty).

claims.claim: Page security settings require Registration and authentication to be implemented on your site.
claims.location: Section: Page access levels, info box (line 29)
claims.evidence: Architectural requirement — admin settings define the model but front-end implementation is required for enforcement. Cannot be directly verified from code alone.

claims.claim: Your development team must also reflect the settings on the front end so that access restrictions take effect on the live site.
claims.location: Section: Page access levels, info box (line 29)
claims.evidence: Same architectural principle — the ContentAccessSettings model is consumed by front-end code. This is a platform design constraint, not directly verifiable from a single source file.

claims.claim: Properties → Security tab exists for web pages.
claims.evidence: WebPageSecurityModel.cs exists at UIPages/WebPages/WebPage/Properties/Security/WebPageSecurityModel.cs, confirming the Security sub-tab within Properties.

claims.claim: To make the page accessible only to signed-in members, select 'Requires authentication'.
claims.location: Section: Configure page security, step 4, bullet 1 (line 42)
claims.evidence: KenticoAdminWebsitesResources.resx key 'websites.webpages.properties.security.issecured' = 'Requires authentication'. WebPageSecurityModel.cs line 19: CheckBoxComponent with this label. Setting IsSecured=true maps to ContentAccessSettings.Secured().
claims.docFact: AREA-012: Localization strings confirm 'Requires authentication' checkbox label

claims.claim: To restrict the page to specific member roles, select 'Requires authentication' and then select one or more roles in the 'Membership roles' selector that appears.
claims.location: Section: Configure page security, step 4, bullet 2 (line 43)
claims.evidence: Resource key 'websites.webpages.properties.security.requiredroles' = 'Membership roles'. WebPageSecurityModel.cs lines 26-33: ObjectIdSelectorComponent with MemberRoleInfo.OBJECT_TYPE and [VisibleIfTrue(nameof(IsSecured))] — selector only appears when checkbox is checked.
claims.docFact: Source: WebPageSecurityModel.cs — IsSecured checkbox + RequiredRoles ObjectIdSelectorComponent with VisibleIfTrue, MaximumItems=0 (unlimited)

claims.claim: If 'Requires authentication' is selected but no member roles are selected, the page is accessible to all signed-in members.
claims.location: Section: Configure page security, info box (line 49)
claims.evidence: Resource key 'websites.webpages.properties.security.requiredroles.tooltip' = 'Restricts access to members with the specified roles. If no roles are selected, the page is accessible to all authenticated members.' Also, WebPageSecurity.cs: IsSecured=true with no roles → ContentAccessSettings.Secured().
claims.docFact: Source: KenticoAdminWebsitesResources.resx — tooltip: 'If no roles are selected, the page is accessible to all authenticated members.'

claims.claim: When you select one or more roles, only members assigned to at least one of those roles can view the page.
claims.location: Section: Configure page security, info box (line 49)
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.SecuredWithRoles() requires ≥1 role ID. The access model restricts to the specified roles. Same tooltip confirms: 'Restricts access to members with the specified roles.'

claims.claim: Select 'Publish change' to save security settings.
claims.location: Section: Configure page security, step 5 (line 53); Section: Restrict a page, step 6 (line 74)
claims.evidence: WebPageSecurity.cs line 100: SubmitConfiguration.Label = PublishedVersionExists(webPageModel) ? 'Publish change' : 'Save change'. Resource key 'websites.webpages.properties.publishchange' = 'Publish change'. Note: label is conditional — shows 'Save change' for unpublished pages.
claims.notes: Conditionally correct. For already-published pages (the common scenario for security changes), the label is 'Publish change'. For never-published drafts, it would say 'Save change'. Previous review noted this as a minor point.

claims.claim: If the page has subpages, a dialog appears asking whether to apply the settings to the current page only or to all pages located under it as well.
claims.evidence: WebPageSecuritySubmitModel.cs line 15: RadioGroupComponent with options CURRENT_PAGE_AND_CHILDREN and CURRENT_PAGE_ONLY. Resource strings: 'Apply to this item and all items located under it' / 'Apply to this item only'. Doc paraphrases 'item' as 'page' for business audience — acceptable since context is explicitly page security.

claims.claim: A lock icon ({% icon xp-lock %}) appears next to the secured page in the content tree, confirming the page requires authentication.
claims.location: Section: Configure page security, result paragraph (line 57)
claims.evidence: BaseContentItemStatusRetriever.cs lines 285-299: AddSecuredIcon() method comment says 'Adds a diamond icon to the status when authentication is required' and uses IconName = Icons.Diamond. Icons.cs line 971: Diamond = 'xp-diamond'. Test assertions confirm: ContentItemStatusRetrieverTests.cs line 523 and WebPageStatusRetrieverTests.cs line 499 both assert: securedIcon.IconName == Icons.Diamond. The Lock icon (xp-lock, Icons.cs line 1727) exists but is NOT used for content tree security status.
claims.correction: The icon is a diamond icon ({% icon xp-diamond %}), not a lock icon. Change: 'A lock icon ({% icon xp-lock %})' → 'A diamond icon ({% icon xp-diamond %})'

claims.claim: Newly created pages inherit access privileges from their parent page.
claims.location: Section: Configure page security, info box (line 62)
claims.evidence: InheritSecurityOptions.cs defines Inherit enum value: 'Inherit the security configuration from the new parent web page item.' CreateWebPageTests.cs test 'Create_ParentHasMemberRoles_MemberRolesAreCopiedToNewPage' confirms that parent page roles are copied to new child pages.

claims.claim: In the 'Membership roles' selector, select one or more member roles (step-by-step for restricting a page).
claims.location: Section: Restrict a page to specific member roles, step 5 (line 73)
claims.evidence: Same UI element verified in claim 8. Resource key 'websites.webpages.properties.security.requiredroles' = 'Membership roles'. ObjectIdSelectorComponent with MaximumItems=0 (unlimited).

claims.claim: Visitors who are not signed in, or who are signed in but not assigned to any of the selected roles, cannot view the page on the live site.
claims.location: Section: Restrict a page, result paragraph (line 76)
claims.evidence: Follows from the three-tier model. SecuredWithRoles requires both authentication AND membership in ≥1 of the specified roles. Non-authenticated visitors and authenticated members without the required roles are excluded.

claims.claim: Open Members → List of membership roles to view, create, or edit roles.
claims.location: Section: Restrict a page, info box (line 80)
claims.evidence: KenticoAdminBaseResources.resx key 'base.memberroles.list.caption' = 'List of membership roles' and key 'base.memberroles.list.headline' = 'List of membership roles'. The UI label is literally 'List of membership roles'.
claims.docFact: AREA-012: Admin UI member role pages already present

claims.claim: When moving a page, a mismatch between access privileges of the moved page and its new parent may occur. Options: Inherit / Maintain current / Cancel.
claims.location: Section: Move pages with access privileges mismatch (lines 86-91)
claims.evidence: InheritSecurityOptions.cs defines: Inherit ('Inherit the security configuration from the new parent web page item') and KeepCurrent ('Maintain the current security configuration'). Cancel is a standard dialog dismiss action. MoveWebPageCommandArguments.cs confirms the property: 'Indicates whether the web page item should inherit security settings from the target web page parent or keep its current settings.'

terminologyChangeVerification.description: Attempt 2 changed 4 instances of choose/chosen to select/selected and 1 instance of 'membership roles' to 'member roles' in prose text. Verified all substitutions.

changes.before: and then choose one or more roles
changes.after: and then select one or more roles
changes.accuracyImpact: none — pure word substitution, no change in technical meaning
none — 'member roles' is the correct prose term per REC-013; UI label 'Membership roles' correctly remains in bold UI references

changes.before: choose one or more member roles
changes.after: select one or more member roles

terminologyChangeVerification.verdict: All terminology changes are accuracy-neutral. No technical meaning altered.

researchRecommendationCompliance.REC-010: Cited and followed. Location-before-action pattern used. Procedures under 8 steps. Finalizing actions explicit.
researchRecommendationCompliance.REC-013: Cited and followed. 'member' for registered visitor, 'sign in' (verb), 'member role' in prose. UI labels ('Membership roles', 'Requires authentication', 'List of membership roles') correctly preserved in bold references.
